sanskrit: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् । न हिनस्त्यात्मनाऽऽत्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
hindiTranslation: क्योंकि सब जगह समरूपसे स्थित ईश्वरको समरूपसे देखनेवाला मनुष्य अपनेआपसे अपनी हिंसा नहीं करता, इसलिये वह परमगतिको प्राप्त हो जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Seeing the divine equally everywhere, a person does not wound the true self by the true self, and reaches the highest state.
essence: Seeing unity everywhere ends the violence that returns to yourself.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Seeing the same divine presence everywhere ends inner self-harm and leads to the highest state.
simpleInsight: When separation drops, self-sabotage loses its grip.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still answering Arjuna on the battlefield, where fear and grief have frozen action. After showing that the same reality stands in every being, he says that one who sees this unity no longer attacks the true self through hatred or confusion.
modernRelevance: You replay a harsh conversation in your head and turn it into self-blame. The moment you stop dividing people into enemies and allies, the inner violence eases.
translationLiteral: समम् (equally) / पश्यन् (seeing) / हि (indeed) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / समवस्थितम् (equally situated) / ईश्वरम् (the divine ruler) / न (not) / हिनस्ति (hurts) / आत्मना (by the true self) / आत्मानम् (the true self) / ततः (therefore) / याति (goes) / पराम् (highest) / गतिम् (state, destination)
insight: It shifts perception from divided beings to one shared presence, ending the reflex to injure yourself through hatred or fear.

quoteVariants.text: See the divine everywhere. You stop harming yourself.
The same divine presence is everywhere.
See one presence in all things.
